topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink swam on the parchment, a damning scarlet blotch across the B-minus. Liam stared at the paper, his knuckles white where he gripped the edge of the desk. His stomach twisted into a tight, cold knot. He reread the feedback, each word landing like a small stone in his chest. He hadn't understood the core concepts; he’d just panicked during the exam. A slow, shallow breath escaped him, and he pressed the heels of his hands against his eyes, trying to physically squeeze the confusing information out of his brain. The silence of his small dorm room amplified the frantic drumming in his ears. He wanted to bolt, to disappear into the anonymity of the campus grounds, but his feet remained rooted to the worn carpet.

[story 2]

“Fifty percent,” the professor had stated, his voice flat and devoid of sympathy. Maya felt a sudden, sharp drop in temperature. She meticulously smoothed the crumpled paper on her lap, her fingers trembling slightly as they traced the low mark. Her jaw tightened until a dull ache resonated through her molars. She began pacing the narrow confines of the library aisle, the rhythmic tap of her worn sneakers against the linoleum a frantic counterpoint to the quiet hum of the study hall. Every passing student seemed to observe her, and the attention felt like a physical weight pressing down on her shoulders. *This isn't right,* she thought, the conviction solidifying into a hard, unpleasant certainty.

[story 3]

Ethan picked at a loose thread on his sleeve, his gaze fixed on the ceiling tiles as if searching for an escape route. The grade—a disheartening C—felt like a physical blow, stealing the air from his lungs. He shifted in his chair, the cheap plastic groaning under his weight, and then abruptly stood up, knocking his chair back with a loud scrape. He walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent sprawl of the city below. His hands were clenched into fists so tight that the skin around his wrists felt tight and constricted. He didn't want to look at his notes; he just wanted to be somewhere else, anywhere but this stark realization of underperformance.
